query_normalized: llmxive follow-up: extending "full attention strikes back: transferring full attention into sparse w" linguistics
rationale: This paper satisfies criteria (a) and (d) as it investigates the same core mechanism (attention manipulation in LLMs) and proposes a foundational method (Scaled ReAttention) for extending context handling, which is directly relevant to the user's goal of transferring full attention capabilities into sparse or efficient architectures for linguistics tasks.
